subcodes.title: Javab-i-Ahadin mina’s-sabirin
subcodes.first_line: Praise be to God, who hath brought into being the contingent beings for the purpose of their acceptance, and hath fashioned them in the form of their obedience. Thereupon, He hath raised them up in the stations of command and prohibition, that He might recompense them according to their true deserts. Thereafter, He hath subjected them to trials, that He might distinguish them. Exalted be He! How wondrous is His trial, and how mighty His proof [4o]
subcodes.first_line_orig: بسم الله الرحمن الرحیم. الحمد لله الذی خلق الممکنات لشأن قبولهم و صورهم علی هیکل طاعتهم ثم قد اقامهم فی مقامات الامر والنهی لیجزیهم وصفهم ثم افتتنهم لیمیزینهم فسبحانه ما اعجب فتنته و اعظم بینته
subcodes.subjects: @Word of God and human limitations, @spiritual emotions and susceptibilities, @power of prayer, @power of the Manifestation of God, @tests and trials; sacrifice and suffering, @servitude; submission to God; repentance, @prayer for forgiveness, @transcendence; unknowability of God
